briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Define canonical HTML tag categories (block, inline, list, table, styled, text) as a lookup dictionary for the HTML reader pipeline.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: HTML tag classification — semantic role assignment for each HTML tag. \par \b Input\/ \: None (pure data class evaluated at import time). \par \b Output\/ \: Module-level class_html_tags with categorized tag name sets. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(8): dedoc.readers.html_reader] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: available_tags ALWAYS contains sorted unique tag names. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why a class with class-level constants instead of a plain dict? A: Class-level constants provide autocomplete-friendly, namespaced access for IDE and grep navigation. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Added SEMANTIC TEMPLATE markup.] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS [10][HTML tag semantic categories] => HtmlTags \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
